claim: Avoid in-memory chat history and vector stores for RAG in n8n; use Supabase with Postgres and pgvector instead for production scalability
actionable: Replace windowed buffer memory and in-memory vector stores with Supabase (SQL+pgvector) for production n8n workflows
source_title: 10 n8n Tips in 10 Minutes to 10x Your AI Automations

claim: For LLM choice in n8n: use Claude 3.5 Sonnet for best quality, Groq with Llama for speed, GPT-4o mini for affordability; use text-embedding-3-small for embeddings
actionable: Set up n8n with Claude 3.5 Sonnet for critical tasks, Groq Llama for real-time needs, and GPT-4o mini for cost-sensitive workflows
source_title: 10 n8n Tips in 10 Minutes to 10x Your AI Automations

claim: Use different extraction nodes in n8n for different file types (PDF vs text vs Excel) and include all needed nodes in your workflow
actionable: When building n8n RAG pipelines, add separate extraction nodes for each file type you plan to ingest
source_title: 10 n8n Tips in 10 Minutes to 10x Your AI Automations

claim: Reference previous node outputs only work for the immediately preceding node; reference earlier nodes by name using syntax like 'node_name.data.field'
actionable: When referencing node outputs, use 'json.field' for the previous node, but use 'node_name.data.field' for any earlier node
source_title: 10 n8n Tips in 10 Minutes to 10x Your AI Automations

claim: Combine webhook and chat triggers in n8n with JavaScript logic to test agents internally while also exposing them as APIs
actionable: Use both webhook trigger and 'chat message received' trigger with conditional logic to test AI agents in n8n UI and serve them as APIs
source_title: 10 n8n Tips in 10 Minutes to 10x Their AI Automations

claim: Use data pinning to persist test execution output so you don't have to re-execute workflows when developing and referencing dynamic values
actionable: Click the pin button in n8n node output to persist test data across page reloads so you can reference it while building downstream nodes
source_title: 10 n8n Tips in 10 Minutes to 10x Your AI Automations

claim: Set up error workflows in n8n with an 'error trigger' and link them in settings so failures are automatically reported (e.g., to Slack)
actionable: Create a separate n8n workflow with an 'error trigger' that sends Slack messages, then link it in your main workflow's error handler settings
source_title: 10 n8n Tips in 10 Minutes to 10x Your AI Automations

claim: Use n8n's schedule trigger for recurring tasks like daily reports that can't be event-driven
actionable: Use schedule triggers for workflows like daily alert generation that need to run on intervals, not in response to events
source_title: 10 n8n Tips in 10 Minutes to 10x Your AI Automations

claim: n8n's workflow library has over 1000 templates; search it for examples before building from scratch
actionable: Check n8n's workflow library (n8n.io/workflows) for templates matching your use case before implementing custom workflows
source_title: 10 n8n Tips in 10 Minutes to 10x Your AI Automations
